description: Manage user-registered notification endpoints and deliver async push notifications to HTTP or email gateways when room events match configurable push rules.

actors.name: User
actors.description: Account owner registering or managing push notification endpoints

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server evaluating push rules and dispatching notifications

actors.name: Push Gateway
actors.description: External HTTP service or email relay receiving notification payloads

actors.name: Identity Server
actors.description: Used to validate that an email push key belongs to the user's account

fields.name: app_id
fields.label: Identifier for the application registering the pusher

fields.name: pushkey
fields.label: Delivery endpoint; a URL for HTTP pushers or an email address for email pushers

fields.name: data
fields.label: Gateway-specific configuration such as endpoint URL and format

fields.name: lang
fields.label: Language code used when generating localised notification content

fields.name: device_id
fields.label: Optional device association for the pusher

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Whether the pusher is currently delivering notifications

values.description: Pusher is registered but temporarily not delivering notifications

transitions.description: User toggles the pusher off without deleting it
Associated device deleted, triggering cascade removal

registration: A pusher is uniquely identified by the combination of user_id, app_id, and pushkey
Email pushers require the pushkey (email address) to be a verified third-party identifier on the account
Push notifications are only delivered when the pusher is in the active state
delivery: Notification delivery is asynchronous and does not block event processing
Push rules are evaluated against each event to determine whether a notification should be sent
Failed HTTP deliveries are retried with exponential backoff
Deleting a device cascades to remove all pushers associated with that device
Pushers are distributed across worker instances using consistent hashing

given: for email pushers: pushkey is a verified identity on the account
pusher with this app_id and pushkey does not already exist

then.description: Pusher record stored and background delivery task started

pusher_registered.result: Device will receive notifications when matching push rules are triggered

given: pusher with this app_id and pushkey already exists for the user
pusher_updated.result: Existing pusher uses new configuration going forward

given: a room event matches at least one active push rule for the user

then.description: Notification payload sent to the external gateway

notification_delivered.result: External gateway receives the notification and forwards to the user's device

given: external gateway returns an error or is unreachable
notification_delivery_failed.result: Delivery retried with backoff; user may not receive the notification if all retries fail

given: user requests to disable the pusher without deleting it
pusher_disabled.result: Pusher stops delivering notifications but remains registered

given: user requests deletion or device cascade triggers removal

then.description: Pusher record removed; delivery task stopped

pusher_deleted.result: No further notifications delivered through this endpoint

given: pushkey is not a verified third-party identifier on the account

errors.message: The email address is not a verified identifier on your account

events.name: pusher.registered
events.description: A new notification endpoint was registered

events.name: pusher.updated
events.description: An existing pusher's configuration was changed

events.name: pusher.disabled
events.description: A pusher was temporarily disabled by the user

events.name: notification.delivered
events.description: A push notification was successfully sent to the external gateway

events.name: notification.delivery_failed
events.description: Push notification delivery failed and is scheduled for retry

related.reason: Pusher deletion is cascaded when the associated device is removed
Email pushers validate their pushkey against verified identities on the account
